data.title: Tetragon Financial Group Limited March 2026 Monthly Factsheet
data.description: In April 2026, Tetragon published their Monthly Factsheet for March, detailing the financial performance and updates. Tetragon Financial Group, a closed-ended investment firm based in Guernsey, shared insights through the Monthly Factsheet. This document provides information about the company's operations and investments during March 2026. Readers can access the detailed report to learn more about Tetragon's activities and financial status.

data.title: 21 Capital soars on planned merger with Strike & Elektron; Tether suggests Elektron CEO as president, with Strike's Mallers in exec role.
data.description: Tether has suggested Raphael Zagury, the founder and CEO of Elektron, to take on the role of president in the potential merged entity. Additionally, Jack Mallers from Strike would also hold a key executive position within the company.
South Korea, Australia to work together to boost energy supply chain resilience, according to the foreign ministry.

data.title: Announcement of the net asset value of INVL Technology as of 31 March 2026
data.description: According to Section XI of the Articles of Association of INVL Technology, the company's net asset value on March 31, 2026, was reported at EUR 65,455,439, which translates to EUR 5.4708 per share.

data.description: In announcement number 35/2026 dated April 30, 2026, a company has released important information.

data.title: Solid financial performance in the first quarter of 2026 driven by good customer activity, lending growth and strong credit quality Net profit of DKK 5.7 billion
data.description: Strong financial results were achieved in the initial quarter of 2026, primarily propelled by increased customer engagement, growth in lending activities, and robust credit quality. A net profit of DKK 5.7 billion was recorded during this period.

data.title: New Classification System of Malocclusions Published in the Journal of Aligner Orthodontics (JAO) by Prof. Gang Shen's Team
data.description: On April 30, 2026 in Shanghai, a groundbreaking study titled "A Novel Classification System for Malocclusions with Facial Convexity" was released in the inaugural issue of the 2026 Journal of Aligner Orthodontics (JAO). The paper, written by Professor Gang, focuses on a new system for categorizing malocclusions associated with facial convexity.
